source.title: Gas Well Blowout and Fire at Pryor Trust Well 1H-9

event.top_event: Gas well blowout and rig fire
actions_taken: Attempt to close blowout preventer after fire started
event.summary: On January 22, 2018, a blowout and rig fire occurred at Pryor Trust Well 1H-9 in Pittsburg County, Oklahoma. The fire killed five workers inside the driller's cabin from thermal burn injuries and smoke/soot inhalation. The blowout occurred approximately 3.5 hours after tripping drill pipe out of the well. Root causes included failure of the primary barrier (hydrostatic pressure from drilling mud) and secondary barrier (human detection of influx and BOP activation). Contributing factors included underbalanced operations performed without proper planning or equipment, inadequate training on electronic trip sheets, equipment misalignment causing data interpretation errors, failure to identify surface pressure, miscalculated weighted pill, deactivated alarm system, lack of flow checks, inadequate driller training in influx detection, and ineffective safety management system. The BOP failed to close after the fire because hydraulic control hoses burned and leaked, depleting accumulator pressure.
recommendations: OSHA should establish drilling-specific regulatory standards for onshore drilling safety
API should develop industry standards for alarm management in drilling operations
API should develop industry guidance on tripping procedures for underbalanced operations
Patterson-UTI should implement effective monitoring of policy implementation including flow check compliance
Patterson-UTI should test drillers' abilities in detecting gas influx indications
Patterson-UTI should improve driller training on electronic trip sheets and data interpretation
Red Mountain Operating should specify required barriers during operations and response procedures if barriers are lost
IADC should develop industry guidance on tripping procedures
Pason Systems should improve alarm system design to alert personnel to hazardous conditions during different operating states
National Oilwell Varco should improve BOP design and hydraulic system protection
State of Oklahoma should establish drilling safety regulations for onshore operations
Design improvements needed to protect driller's cabin occupants and rig floor personnel with safe escape routes
key_phrases: Underbalanced Operations Performed Without Proper Planning, Procedures, or Needed Equipment
Signs of Influx Either Not Identified or Inadequately Responded To
BOP Could Not Close Due to Burned Hydraulic Hoses
Lack of Safety Requirements by Regulation

threats.name: Loss of hydrostatic pressure barrier during underbalanced operations
Gas influx into wellbore during tripping operations
Failure to detect gas influx at surface
Swabbing effect reducing wellbore pressure during pipe removal
Inadequate mud weight and weighted pill miscalculation

consequences.name: Fatalities from thermal burns and smoke inhalation
Loss of containment and environmental release

controls.name: Hydrostatic pressure barrier from drilling mud column
controls.barrier_role: Primary barrier to prevent gas influx

supporting_text: The cause of the blowout and rig fire was the failure of both the primary barrier—hydrostatic pressure produced by drilling mud
Underbalanced drilling was performed without needed planning, equipment, skills, or procedures, thus nullifying the planned primary barrier to prevent gas influx

controls.name: Weighted pill to overbalance well
controls.barrier_role: Restore hydrostatic pressure after underbalanced operations
controls.lod_basis: Well control procedure to regain overbalanced conditions

supporting_text: A weighted pill intended to overbalance the well was apparently miscalculated. After pill placement, the well was still underbalanced

controls.name: Flow checks to detect gas influx
controls.barrier_role: Detect formation fluid entry into wellbore

supporting_text: Key flow checks to determine if the well was flowing were not performed before the incident
Drilling rig workers performed very few of the company-required flow checks during the drilling of well 1H-9 and the previous well
The drilling contractor did not effectively monitor the implementation rate of its flow check policy

controls.name: Driller monitoring of pit gains and flow indicators
controls.barrier_role: Detect gas influx through visual observation of mud pit levels and flow line indicators

supporting_text: Equipment was aligned differently than normal during the tripping operation, leading to confusion in interpreting the well data which caused rig workers to miss indications of the gas influx
The driller was not effectively trained in using a new electronic trip sheet, which is used to help monitor for gas influx
Driller and Company Man Had Difficulties Interpreting Data Due to Mud Bucket Alignment

controls.name: Alarm system for abnormal well conditions
controls.barrier_role: Alert personnel to hazardous conditions including gas influx indications
controls.lod_basis: Safety instrumented system for well monitoring

supporting_text: Both the day and night driller chose to turn off the entire alarm system, contributing to both drillers missing critical indications of the gas influx and imminent blowout
Alarm System Off

The alarm system also was not effectively designed to alert personnel to hazardous conditions during different operating states (e.g., drilling, tripping, circulating, and surface operations) and would have sounded excessive non-critical alarms during the 14 hours leading to the blowout, which likely led to the drillers choosing to turn off the alarm system

controls.name: Electronic trip sheet for monitoring well data during tripping
controls.barrier_role: Assist driller in monitoring for gas influx during pipe removal operations

supporting_text: The driller was not effectively trained in using a new electronic trip sheet, which is used to help monitor for gas influx
Driller Had Difficulties with Electronic Trip Sheet

controls.name: Blowout preventer (BOP) closure
controls.barrier_role: Contain well and prevent uncontrolled release of formation fluids
controls.lod_basis: Primary containment device for well control

supporting_text: The blowout preventer also failed to close when its activation was attempted after the fire started
BOP Could Not Close Due to Burned Hydraulic Hoses

The CSB determined the BOP did not function likely because the control hoses that supplied hydraulic fluid to the BOP to function the rams had burned in the fire and leaked the hydraulic control fluid, soon depleting the accumulator stored pressure to the point the blowout preventer could not be closed

controls.name: Accumulator for BOP hydraulic pressure
controls.barrier_role: Store hydraulic pressure to operate blowout preventer rams

supporting_text: the control hoses that supplied hydraulic fluid to the BOP to function the rams had burned in the fire and leaked the hydraulic control fluid, soon depleting the accumulator stored pressure to the point the blowout preventer could not be closed

controls.name: Tripping procedure for underbalanced well conditions
controls.barrier_role: Provide guidance for safe pipe removal from underbalanced well

supporting_text: Underbalanced Operations Performed Without Proper Planning, Procedures, or Needed Equipment
Tripping was performed out of the underbalanced well, which allowed a large amount of gas to enter the well
Need for Industry Tripping Guidance

controls.name: Management of Change (MOC) process for operational deviations
controls.barrier_role: Control and manage deviations from planned drilling operations

supporting_text: Management of Change Ineffective for Operational Changes
RMO and Patterson Attempted an Unplanned Underbalanced Operation
The operating company did not specify the barriers required during operations, or how to respond if a barrier was lost

controls.name: Driller training and competency assessment for influx detection
controls.barrier_role: Ensure drillers can identify and respond to gas influx indications

supporting_text: The drilling contractor did not test its drillers' abilities in detecting indications of gas influx through, for example, simulated pit gains

The absence of testing drillers' influx detection skills—a safety-critical aspect of well control—might have contributed to both drillers not detecting the significant gas influx leading to the blowout

controls.name: Surface pressure identification before BOP operations
controls.barrier_role: Detect and account for surface pressure before opening BOP

supporting_text: Surface pressure was not identified two separate times before opening the BOP during operations before the blowout, when there was evidently pressure at the surface of the well
Incorrect Determination That There Was No Surface Pressure Before Opening BOP

controls.name: Driller's cabin design and safe escape routes
controls.barrier_role: Protect personnel from fire and provide safe escape

supporting_text: The victims had no safe escape route from the driller's cabin (dog house) once the drilling mud and gas ignited
The workers were effectively trapped once the fire started
Driller's Cabin Design

controls.name: Safety management system and policy implementation monitoring
controls.barrier_role: Ensure effective implementation of safety policies and procedures

supporting_text: The safety management system in place was not effective for managing safe rig operations
Patterson Did Not Effectively Monitor the Implementation of its Policies
Gaps in Safety Management System

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
